name: Roast Veal Brisket with Marsala-Mushroom Sauc
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces Dried porcini mushrooms, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Season veal generously with dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a heavy large skillet over high heat. Add onions and sauté until brown, about 15 minutes. Sprinkle flour over and stir for an additional 3 minutes.
4. Transfer the onion mixture to a large roasting pan, spreading onions in the center and bones around the edge. Add one brisket to the skillet and cook until brown on all sides, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer the brisket to the roasting pan and place atop the onions.
5. Repeat with the second brisket. Add button mushrooms, stock, Marsala wine, dried porcini mushrooms, garlic, and allspice to the skillet. Bring the mixture to a boil, scraping up any browned bits.
6. Pour the contents of the skillet over the briskets in the roasting pan. Cover tightly with heavy-duty foil. Roast until the briskets are very tender, about 2 hours.
7. Uncover and let cool for at least 30 minutes. Transfer the briskets to a platter. Spoon the onions into a strainer set over a large saucepan and press hard to release juices. Discard the onions in the strainer. Set the mushrooms aside.
8. Strain the pan juices from the roasting pan into the same large saucepan. Spoon the fat from the top of the pan juices and discard. Boil until the pan juices coat the spoon lightly, about 20 minutes. Return the reserved mushrooms to the sauce and season to taste with salt and pepper.
9. Cut the briskets diagonally across the grain into thin slices; trim excess fat if desired. Overlap the slices in a large casserole dish. Spoon the mushroom sauce over the top. (This recipe can be prepared up to 3 days ahead. Cover tightly and refrigerate. To reheat, cover and warm covered in a 350°F oven for about 45 minutes before continuing.)
10. Garnish with fresh thyme if desired and serve.
